verses.1: Let me sing for my well beloved a song of my beloved about his vineyard.
verses.3: “Now, inhabitants of Jerusalem and men of Judah,
verses.4: What could have been done more to my vineyard, that I have not done in it?
verses.5: Now I will tell you what I will do to my vineyard.
verses.6: I will lay it a wasteland.
verses.7: For the vineyard of the LORD of Armies is the house of Israel,
verses.8: Woe to those who join house to house,
verses.9: In my ears, the LORD of Armies says: “Surely many houses will be desolate,
verses.10: For ten acres of vineyard shall yield one bath,
verses.11: Woe to those who rise up early in the morning, that they may follow strong drink,
verses.12: The harp, lyre, tambourine, and flute, with wine, are at their feasts;
verses.13: Therefore my people go into captivity for lack of knowledge.
verses.14: Therefore Sheol has enlarged its desire,
verses.16: but the LORD of Armies is exalted in justice,
verses.17: Then the lambs will graze as in their pasture,
verses.18: Woe to those who draw iniquity with cords of falsehood,
verses.19: who say, “Let him make haste, let him hasten his work, that we may see it;
verses.20: Woe to those who call evil good, and good evil;
verses.21: Woe to those who are wise in their own eyes,
verses.22: Woe to those who are mighty to drink wine,
verses.23: who acquit the guilty for a bribe,
verses.24: Therefore as the tongue of fire devours the stubble,
verses.25: Therefore the LORD’s anger burns against his people,
verses.26: He will lift up a banner to the nations from far away,
verses.27: No one shall be weary nor stumble among them;
verses.29: Their roaring will be like a lioness.
verses.30: They will roar against them in that day like the roaring of the sea.
